title: 2009 St Helens R.F.C. season
wikitext:
{{Update|date=March 2026|reason=Seems to have not been edited much since 2009.}}{{Tone|date=March 2026}}{{Infobox rugby league team season
 | year            = 2009
 | prev_season     = [[2008 St Helens RLFC season|2008]]
 | next_season     = [[2010 St Helens RLFC season|2010]]
}}

[[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] entered their 137th year in 2009, and were in contention for of [[rugby league]]'s [[Super League XIV|Super League]] and [[2009 Challenge Cup|Carnegie Challenge Cup]] competitions.

==2009 season==
===[[Super League XIV]]===
====[[Super League XIV Results#Round 1|Round 1]]====
Saints came back from a 14-nil deficit early in the second half to beat [[Warrington Wolves]] 26-14.<ref name="Saints vs. Warrington">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086251|title=St Helens vs. Warrington match report|publisher=engagesuperleague.com|date=2009-06-22|access-date=2009-06-22}}</ref> [[Image:Matt Gidley.jpg|thumb|130px|right|[[Matt Gidley]] scored twice against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]].]]

====[[Super League XIV Results#Round 2|Round 2]]====
Saints turned over the much improved from last season [[Huddersfield Giants]] 23-6.<ref name="Saints vs. Huddersfield">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086257|title=St Helens vs. Huddersfield match report|publisher=engagesuperleague.com|date=2009-06-22|access-date=2009-06-22}}</ref>

====[[Super League XIV Results#Round 3|Round 3]]====
[[Hull Kingston Rovers]] inflicted Saints' first defeat of the season, with [[Michael Dobson (rugby league)|Michael Dobson]] scoring a late penalty goal to give the Robins a 20-19 win.<ref name="Saints vs. Hull KR">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086264|title=St Helens vs. Hull KR match report|publisher=engagesuperleague.com|date=2009-06-22|access-date=2009-06-22}}</ref>

====[[Super League XIV Results#Round 4|Round 4]]====
Saints scraped past newly promoted [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders]] in the lowest scoring game in the [[Super League]]; [[Tom Armstrong (rugby league)|Tom Armstrong]] scoring the only try in a 4-nil win.<ref name="Saints vs. Celtic">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086272|title=St Helens vs. Celtic match report|publisher=engagesuperleague.com|date=2009-06-22|access-date=2009-06-22}}</ref>

====[[Super League XIV Results#Round 5|Round 5]]====
Saints brushed aside the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] by 38 points to 12.<ref name="Saints vs. Salford">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086277|title=St Helens vs. Salford match report|publisher=engagesuperleague.com|date=2009-06-22|access-date=2009-06-22}}</ref>

====[[Super League XIV Results#Round 6|Round 6]]====
Saints edged a heated epic with [[Leeds Rhinos]] - winning 26-18 at [[Knowsley Road]].<ref name="Saints vs. Leeds">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086285|title=St Helens vs. Leeds match report|publisher=engagesuperleague.com|date=2009-06-22|access-date=2009-06-22}}</ref> [[Image:Flannery & Galea.jpg|thumb|130px|right|[[Chris Flannery (rugby league)|Chris Flannery]] was man-of-the-match against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].]]

====[[Super League XIV Results#Round 7|Round 7]]====
Saints hammered [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] by 42-18. The Wildcats are in mourning for reserve player [[Leon Walker]], who died in a reserve game.<ref name="Saints vs. Wakefield">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3089715|title=St Helens vs. Wakefield match report|publisher=engagesuperleague.com|date=2009-06-22|access-date=2009-06-22}}</ref>

====[[Super League XIV Results#Round 8|Round 8]]====
Saints scraped past a fired up [[Wigan Warriors]] outfit, winning 19-12 after being 6-nil and 12-6 down.<ref name="Saints vs. Wigan">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086298|title=St Helens vs. Wigan match report|publisher=engagesuperleague.com|date=2009-06-22|access-date=2009-06-22}}</ref>

====[[Super League XIV Results#Round 9|Round 9]]====
Saints bounced [[Hull F.C.]] in the second game of the Easter period by 44-22.<ref name="Saints vs. Hull">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086307|title=St Helens vs. Hull match report|publisher=engagesuperleague.com|date=2009-06-22|access-date=2009-06-22}}</ref>

====[[Super League XIV Results#Round 10|Round 10]]====
Saints became the first team in Super League XIV to surpass 60 points, winning 68-22 at [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]]. [[Tony Puletua]] scored 4 tries but the game is halted for 30 minutes as [[Joe Westerman]] suffered a serious head injury.<ref name="Saints vs. Castleford">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086317|title=St Helens vs. Castleford match report|publisher=engagesuperleague.com|date=2009-06-22|access-date=2009-06-22}}</ref>

====[[Super League XIV Results#Round 11|Round 11]]====
Saints suffered only their second defeat of the season as [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] come back to win 34-30.<ref name="Saints vs. Bradford">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086320|title=St Helens vs. Bradford match report|publisher=engagesuperleague.com|date=2009-06-22|access-date=2009-06-22}}</ref>

====[[Super League XIV Results#Round 12|Round 12]]====
Saints produced arguably their worst display of the campaign, losing 38-18 to arch rivals Wigan at the [[Magic Weekend]].<ref name="Saints vs. Wigan Magic Weekend">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086331|title=St Helens vs. Wigan match report 2|publisher=engagesuperleague.com|date=2009-06-22|access-date=2009-06-22}}</ref>

====[[Super League XIV Results#Round 13|Round 13]]====
Saints defeated [[Catalans Dragons]] for the second time in a week, by coming back from 22-10 down to win 32-28 to move them back to the top of the [[Super League XIV#Table|table]].<ref name="Saints vs. Catalans">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086334|title=St Helens vs. Catalans match report|publisher=engagesuperleague.com|date=2009-06-22|access-date=2009-06-22| archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20090520015341/http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086334| archive-date= 20 May 2009 <!--DASHBot-->| url-status= live}}</ref>

====[[Super League XIV Results#Round 14|Round 14]]====
Saints survived a second-half rally from [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]] to win 22-12.<ref name="Saints vs. Harlequins">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086341|title=St Helens vs. Harlequins match report|publisher=engagesuperleague.com|date=2009-06-22|access-date=2009-06-22| archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20090601191929/http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086341| archive-date= 1 June 2009 <!--DASHBot-->| url-status= live}}</ref>

====[[Super League XIV Results#Round 15|Round 15]]====
Saints eased past the challenge of [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]], winning 30-6.<ref name="Saints vs. Hull Easter">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086346|title=St Helens vs. Hull match report 2|publisher=engagesuperleague.com|date=2009-06-22|access-date=2009-06-22}}</ref>

====[[Super League XIV Results#Round 16|Round 16]]====
Saints thrashed Castleford for a second time this season in a 50 points to 10 success.<ref name="Saints vs. Castleford 2">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086354|title=St Helens vs. Castleford match report 2|publisher=engagesuperleague.com|date=2009-06-22|access-date=2009-06-22| archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20090619130838/http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086354| archive-date= 19 June 2009 <!--DASHBot-->| url-status= live}}</ref>

====[[Super League XIV Results#Round 17|Round 17]]====
Saints set a new record of 16 consecutive away wins in Super League after cantering to a 44-18 success over [[Bradford Bulls]].<ref name="Saints vs. Bradford 2">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086367|title=St Helens vs. Bradford match report 2|publisher=engagesuperleague.com|date=2009-06-22|access-date=2009-06-22| archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20090623062751/http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086367| archive-date= 23 June 2009 <!--DASHBot-->| url-status= live}}</ref>

====[[Super League XIV Results#Round 18|Round 18]]====
Saints shut out the Celtic Crusaders for a second time in the season - in a 30-0 win.<ref name="Saints vs. Celtic 2">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086374|title=St Helens vs. Celtic match report 2|publisher=engagesuperleague.com|date=2009-06-26|access-date=2009-06-26| archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20090628052706/http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086374| archive-date= 28 June 2009 <!--DASHBot-->| url-status= live}}</ref>

====[[Super League XIV Results#Round 19|Round 19]]====
Saints lost their fourth game of the season in a shock 20-10 loss to Salford.<ref name="Saints vs. Salford 2">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086380|title=St Helens vs. Salford match report 2|publisher=engagesuperleague.com|date=2009-07-06|access-date=2009-07-06}}</ref>

====[[Super League XIV Results#Round 20|Round 20]]====
Saints beat neighbours Warrington for the second time in the league, in another fantastic game, this time by a margin of 40-26.
<ref name="Saints vs. Warrington 2">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3148640|title=St Helens vs. Warrington match report 2|publisher=engagesuperleague.com|date=2009-07-11|access-date=2009-07-13}}</ref>

====[[Super League XIV Results#Round 21|Round 21]]====
Despite outscoring their opponents Wakefield by 5 tries to four, Saints crash to their second defeat in three games, losing 22-20.<ref name="Saints vs. Wakefield 2">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086403|title=St Helens vs. Wakefield match report 2|publisher=engagesuperleague.com|date=2009-07-11|access-date=2009-07-19}}</ref>

====[[Super League XIV Results#Round 22|Round 22]]====
Saints bounce back from two defeats in three games with a convincing 44-24 win at Harlequins to take them back to two points clear at the top of the table.<ref name="Saints vs. Harlequins 2">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086411|title=St Helens vs. Harlequins match report 2|publisher=engagesuperleague.co.uk|date=2009-07-25|access-date=2009-07-26}}</ref>

====[[Super League XIV Results#Round 23|Round 23]]====
Saints edge a third game of the season against Wigan, with [[Kyle Eastmond]] scoring a late try to secure a 10-6 victory.<ref name="Saints vs. Wigan 3">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=2086421|title=St Helens vs. Wigan match report 3|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=2009-07-31|access-date=2009-08-02}}</ref>

====[[Super League XIV Results#Round 24|Round 24]]====
Saints lost their third game in six as they crashed to a 26-10 loss at Hull KR.<ref name="Saints vs. Hull KR 2">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086430|title=St Helens vs. Hull KR match report 2|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=2009-08-16|access-date=2009-08-21}}</ref>

====[[Super League XIV Results#Round 25|Round 25]]====
Another below-par performance saw Saints scrape past a youthful Huddersfield outfit by 12 points to 10, after being 10 points down.<ref name="Saints vs. Huddersfield 2">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086434|title=St Helens vs. Huddersfield match report 2|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=2009-08-21|access-date=2009-08-22}}</ref>

====[[Super League XIV Results#Round 26|Round 26]]====
In a much improved performance, Saints were edged out by bitter rivals Leeds 18-10 in a third superb encounter of the season to all but end their hopes of a fifth consecutive [[League Leader's Shield]].<ref name="Saints vs. Leeds 2">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086443|title=St Helens vs. Leeds match report 2|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=2009-09-04|access-date=2009-09-04}}</ref>

====[[Super League XIV Results#Round 27|Round 27]]====
Saints were beaten by Catalans in the last game of the regular season to finish second in the league ladder behind Leeds.<ref name="Saints vs. Catalans 2">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3086452|title=St Helens vs. Catalans match report 2|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=2009-09-11|access-date=2009-09-19}}</ref>

===[[Challenge Cup 2009]]===
====[[Challenge Cup 2009#Round 4|Round 4]]====
Saints edged past a second epic in two weeks against Leeds in a bruising encounter - pulling through 22-18.<ref name="Saints vs. Leeds Challenge Cup">{{cite web|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/results/117|title=St Helens vs. Leeds CC match report|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=2009-06-22|access-date=2009-06-22}}</ref>

====[[Challenge Cup 2009#Round 5|Round 5]]====
Saints thrashe [[Catalans Dragons]] 42-8 to reach the [[Challenge Cup 2009#Quarterfinals|Quarter Finals]].<ref name="Saints vs. Catalans Challenge Cup">{{cite web|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/results/118|title=St Helens vs. Catalans CC match report|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=2009-06-22|access-date=2009-06-22}}</ref>

====[[Challenge Cup 2009#Quarterfinals|Quarter-Finals]]====
Saints trounced a spirited [[Newcastle Thunder|Gateshead Thunder]] side 66-6 to advance to a record ninth consecutive [[Challenge Cup 2009#Semifinals|Semi-Final]].<ref name="Saints vs. Gateshead Challenge Cup">{{cite web|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/results/119|title=St Helens vs. Gateshead CC match report|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=2009-06-22|access-date=2009-06-22}}</ref>

====[[Challenge Cup 2009#Semifinals|Semi-Finals]]====
Despite a [[Francis Meli]] hat-trick of tries, the holders Saints were beaten 24-14 by Huddersfield.<ref name="Saints vs. Huddersfield Challenge Cup">{{cite web|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/results/120|title=St Helens vs. Huddersfield CC match report|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=2009-08-09|access-date=2009-08-10}}</ref>

===[[Super League XIV#Play-offs|Play-Off Series]]===
====[[2009 Super League season results#Qualifying and Elimination Finals|Week 1]]====
Saints beat Huddersfield in Week One to progress to the qualifying round in a 15-2 success.<ref name="Saints vs. Huddersfield Play-Off">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3195953|title=St Helens vs. Huddersfield P-O match report|publisher=engagesuperleague.com|date=2009-06-22|access-date=2009-06-22}}</ref>

====[[2009 Super League season results#Semi-finals|Week 3]]====
Saints reached a fourth consecutive [[Super League Grand Final]] after beating Wigan in a thrilling 14-10 win.<ref name="Saints vs. Wigan Play-Off">{{cite web|url=http://superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3202308|title=St Helens vs. Wigan P-O match report|publisher=engagesuperleague.com|date=2009-10-03|access-date=2009-10-03| archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20091005065505/http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3202308| archive-date= 5 October 2009 <!--DASHBot-->| url-status= live}}</ref> They will take on Leeds for a third consecutive time at [[Old Trafford (football ground)|Old Trafford]].

====[[2009 Super League Grand Final|Super League Grand Final]]====
Saints lost the grand final to Leeds Rhinos 18-10, this being the third consecutive Grand Final they lose to Leeds.

===Upcoming Fixture(s)===

Saints will round off their [[Super League XIV]] campaign against [[Leeds Rhinos]] at the [[2009 Super League Grand Final]].

===News===

It has been announced that, for the 2010 Super League Season, Saints have signed [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] [[2008 Rugby League World Cup|World Cup]] winning {{rlp|ce}} [[Iosia Soliola]] from the [[Sydney City Roosters]] on a three-year deal.<ref name="Soliola a Saint">{{cite web|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/news/1773|title=Soliola a Saint|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=2009-06-22|access-date=2009-06-22| archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20090515024845/http://www.saintsrlfc.com/news/1773| archive-date= 15 May 2009 <!--DASHBot-->| url-status= live}}</ref>  Additionally, [[Rugby league positions#Scrum half|Scrum-half]] [[Sean Long]] will be leaving [[Knowsley Road]] after a 12-year stint. He is joining [[Hull F.C.]] on a two-year contract.<ref name="Hull Snare Long">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=14268|title=Hull Snare Long|publisher=engagesuperleague.com|date=2009-06-22|access-date=2009-06-22}}</ref> [[Lee Gilmour]] is also on his way out of St Helens. The [[Rugby league positions#Second row|second-row]] has agreed a two-year deal with [[Huddersfield Giants]] for the 2010 season.<ref name="Gilmour to join Giants">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=14675|title=Gilmour to join Giants|publisher=engagesuperleague.com|date=2009-07-14|access-date=2009-07-15}}</ref> Young [[Rugby league positions#Scrum half|half-back]] [[Matty Smith (rugby league)|Matty Smith]] will join [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] for the [[Super League XV|2010 season]] in a loan agreement.<ref name="Smith Switches to Reds">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=15235|title=Smith Switches to Reds|publisher=engagesuperleague.com|date=2009-09-04|access-date=2009-09-04}}</ref> [[Rugby league positions#Prop forward|Prop-forward]] [[Jason Cayless]] will be leaving Saints too after 4 seasons with the club owing to a shoulder injury. He will return to Australia.<ref name="Cayless quits Saints">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_5570353,00.html|title=Cayless quits Saints|publisher=skysports.com|date=2009-09-19|access-date=2009-09-19}}</ref> [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] [[Rugby league positions#Prop forward|prop]] [[Nick Fozzard]] will re-join Saints after being released from the final year of his Rovers contract.<ref name="Fozzard returns to Saints">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=15387 |title=Fozzard returns to Saints |publisher=superleague.com |date=2009-09-20 |access-date=2009-09-20 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090923200751/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=15387 |archive-date=2009-09-23 }}</ref>
[[File:Iosia.jpg|thumb|130px|center|[[Iosia Soliola]] will be on board at the [[Knowsley Road|GPW]] next season.]]
